title: SwiftData Basics
description: Learn the fundamentals of SwiftData including the @Model macro, ModelContainer, and ModelContext for modern data persistence in Swift.

sections.content:
## Introduction to SwiftData

SwiftData is Apple's modern persistence framework built on top of Core Data, designed to work seamlessly with Swift concurrency and the Swift type system. It replaces the heavy boilerplate of Core Data with a clean, macro-driven API.

### The @Model Macro

At the heart of SwiftData is the `@Model` macro. Instead of defining a `.xcdatamodeld` file and generating `NSManagedObject` subclasses, you simply annotate a Swift class:

```swift
@Model
class Book {
    var title: String
    var author: String
    var pageCount: Int
    var isRead: Bool
}
```

The `@Model` macro automatically generates:
- Persisted properties (all stored properties become persisted by default)
- An underlying `@PersistedProperty` wrapper for change tracking
- Conformance to `Observable` and `PersistentModel`
- Initializers for creating and fetching instances

### What Happens Under the Hood

When the compiler processes `@Model`, it expands the class to include persistent property wrappers, change observation, and the glue code needed by the persistence layer. You write plain Swift; the macro handles the rest.

sections.title: Defining a SwiftData Model
ModelContainer Configuration
sections.starterCode:
import Foundation

// Simulating container configuration
// In real SwiftData, you'd use: ModelContainer(for: Book.self)

let schemaTypes = ["Book", "Author", "Library"]
let isStoredInMemoryOnly = false

print("Schema types: \(schemaTypes.joined(separator: ", "))")
print("In-memory only: \(isStoredInMemoryOnly)")
print("Container configured for \(schemaTypes.count) models")
sections.expectedOutput:
Schema types: Book, Author, Library
In-memory only: false
Container configured for 3 models
sections.solution:
import Foundation

let schemaTypes = ["Book", "Author", "Library"]
let isStoredInMemoryOnly = false

print("Schema types: \(schemaTypes.joined(separator: ", "))")
print("In-memory only: \(isStoredInMemoryOnly)")
print("Container configured for \(schemaTypes.count) models")

sections.question: What does the @Model macro automatically generate for a Swift class?
options: Persisted property wrappers, Observable conformance, and PersistentModel conformance
sections.explanation: The @Model macro generates persisted property wrappers, Observable conformance, PersistentModel conformance, and the necessary initializers — all from a plain Swift class.

sections.question: How do you make a ModelContainer available to SwiftUI views?
options: Pass it as a parameter to every view
Create a singleton and access it globally
sections.explanation: The .modelContainer(for:) scene modifier creates the container and injects the ModelContext into the SwiftUI environment, making it accessible via @Environment(\.modelContext).
